report_name: New TCLBANKER Trojan Spreads via WhatsApp and Outlook, Targeting 59 Brazilian Financial Apps

threat_actors.name: Water Saci
threat_actors.description: A threat actor associated with the 'Maverick' malware family and its evolution, TCLBANKER. This group targets the Brazilian financial sector.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The attack chain begins when a user is lured into downloading and executing a malicious MSI installer contained within a ZIP file.

attack_patterns.name: DLL Side-Loading
attack_patterns.description: The malware's MSI installer drops a legitimate, signed executable from Logitech ('Logi AI Prompt Builder.exe') and a malicious DLL, which is loaded by the legitimate program to execute malicious code.

attack_patterns.name: Virtualization/Sandbox Evasion
attack_patterns.description: The TCLBANKER loader incorporates a 'watchdog subsystem' to continuously scan for and evade sandboxes and analysis tools.

attack_patterns.name: Debugger Evasion
attack_patterns.description: TCLBANKER's loader actively scans for debuggers to avoid analysis.

attack_patterns.name: System Checks
attack_patterns.description: The malware confirms the system's default language is Brazilian Portuguese ('LANG_PORTUGUESE_BRAZILIAN') before deploying its final payload.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Service
attack_patterns.description: A worm component hijacks the victim's active WhatsApp Web and Microsoft Outlook applications to send phishing messages with the trojanized installer to the victim's contacts.

malware.name: TCLBANKER
malware.description: A highly sophisticated Brazilian banking trojan that targets 59 financial platforms. It is an evolution of the 'Maverick' family and uses a worm component to spread via WhatsApp and Outlook.

malware.name: Maverick
malware.description: A malware family associated with the threat actor Water Saci, from which the TCLBANKER trojan evolved.

malware.name: SORVEPOTEL
malware.description: A worm whose functionality was evolved and integrated into the TCLBANKER trojan for propagation.

tools.name: Logi AI Prompt Builder
tools.description: A legitimate, signed program from Logitech that is abused by the TCLBANKER installer via DLL side-loading to execute malicious code.

tools.name: WhatsApp
tools.description: The messaging application whose active web session is hijacked by TCLBANKER's worm component to spread phishing messages to the victim's contacts.

tools.name: Microsoft Outlook
tools.description: The desktop email client hijacked by TCLBANKER's worm component to send phishing messages containing the trojanized installer to the victim's contacts.

identities.name: Brazilian Financial Platforms
identities.description: A class of 59 different banking, fintech, and cryptocurrency platforms in Brazil targeted by the TCLBANKER trojan.

identities.name: Logitech
identities.description: Vendor of the legitimate 'Logi AI Prompt Builder' application that is abused via DLL side-loading in the TCLBANKER attack chain.

indicators.name: TCLBANKER Abused Logitech Executable
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: TCLBANKER Abused Logitech Executable
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a90-8bcd-ef1234567890
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of 'Logi AI Prompt Builder.exe', which is abused by the TCLBANKER trojan via DLL side-loading. This rule should be tuned to filter for executions outside of legitimate Logitech software installation paths.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/tclbanker-banking-trojan-targets.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-07
tags:
  - attack.t1574.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\Logi AI Prompt Builder.exe'
  filter_legit:
    Image|startswith: 'C:\Program Files\Logi\'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'Logi AI Prompt Builder.exe', a legitimate application abused by TCLBANKER for DLL side-loading.

indicators.name: Suspicious DLL Loaded by Logi AI Prompt Builder
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious DLL Loaded by Logi AI Prompt Builder
id: 8c3d4e5f-a7b8-4012-8def-123456789012
status: experimental
description: Detects the 'Logi AI Prompt Builder.exe' process loading an unsigned DLL, a behavior associated with the TCLBANKER trojan's DLL side-loading technique.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/tclbanker-banking-trojan-targets.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-07
tags:
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\Logi AI Prompt Builder.exe'
    Signed: false
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the legitimate 'Logi AI Prompt Builder.exe' loading an unsigned DLL, which is indicative of the DLL side-loading technique used by TCLBANKER.

indicators.name: TCLBANKER Outlook Worm Propagation
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential TCLBANKER Outlook Worm Propagation
id: 9e4f5a6b-c9d0-4234-8f01-345678901234
status: experimental
description: Detects command-line execution of Outlook to create and attach a file to a new message, a pattern used by the TCLBANKER worm component for propagation.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/tclbanker-banking-trojan-targets.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-07
tags:
  - attack.t1598.003
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\outlook.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '/c ipm.note'
      - '/a '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of Outlook command-line arguments to automatically create a new message with an attachment, a technique used by the TCLBANKER worm to spread.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious DLL Loaded by Logi AI Prompt Builder
